sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: KAA Gent are in solid form, remaining unbeaten in their last 3 matches with impressive wins like a 4-0 against Standard Liège and a 4-2 against RSC Anderlecht. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, however, are struggling, having failed to win in their last 6 matches, with draws and losses dominating their recent record.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Oud-Heverlee Leuven dominated with a surprising 4-0 win in November 2025, while the other match ended in a 0-0 draw. Despite this, historical data shows KAA Gent with a slight edge in overall matchups with 3 wins to Leuven's 1 in the last 5 head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: KAA Gent, sitting 5th with 33 points, boast a strong attack with 37 goals (3rd in the league), led by Wilfried Kanga (8 goals). Oud-Heverlee Leuven, languishing in 15th with 22 points, have scored only 21 goals, reflecting their offensive struggles and defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Gent, Wilfried Kanga and Maksim Paskotsi are in fine form, with Kanga scoring 4 goals in the last 5 matches. Leuven will rely on Mathieu Maertens, who has a history of performing well against Gent, though their current squad lacks standout in-form players.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Gent's superior form, league position, and attacking prowess, coupled with Sofascore's 61% probability of a home win, they are clear favorites at the Planet Group Arena. Leuven's poor recent record and lack of firepower suggest they will struggle to match Gent's intensity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Oud-Heverlee Leuven holds a commanding 2-0 lead over KAA Gent at the Planet Group Arena. The away side struck early with a headed goal in the 17th minute, followed by a penalty conversion in the 25th minute, capitalizing on their clinical finishing. Gent, despite dominating possession and creating numerous entries into the final third, have failed to convert their chances into goals, leaving them with a mountain to climb in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: KAA Gent controlled much of the ball in the first half, spending significant time in the opposition’s half and making frequent entries into the penalty area. However, their inability to create big chances and convert opportunities has left them vulnerable. On the other hand, Oud-Heverlee Leuven made the most of their fewer attacking moments, efficiently turning their big chances into goals. Leuven also showed strength in aerial duels, winning a higher percentage compared to Gent, which contributed to their first goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw momentum swing between the two sides. Leuven started aggressively, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes before Gent responded with a sustained spell of pressure around the middle of the half. The tide turned back in Leuven’s favor during key moments leading to their goals, where they displayed sharp attacking intent. Towards the end of the half, Gent regained control with a prolonged period of dominance in the opposition’s territory, but they couldn’t break through Leuven’s defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Oud-Heverlee Leuven, Siebe Schrijvers and Roggerio Nyakossi have been pivotal, with both finding the net in the first half. Their ability to capitalize on limited chances highlights their threat going forward. On Gent’s side, players like Abdelkahar Kadri and Tiago Araújo showed creativity with key passes, and they will be crucial if Gent are to mount a comeback. Wilfried Kanga, despite not scoring yet, remains a potential danger man up front for the home side.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for the second half
halftimeSections.text: KAA Gent are likely to come out with renewed urgency in the second half, pushing for an early goal to get back into the game. They may focus on creating more direct chances in the box, leveraging their possession advantage. Oud-Heverlee Leuven, with a two-goal cushion, could adopt a more defensive posture, looking to protect their lead while seeking counter-attacking opportunities. Their aerial strength might again play a role if they exploit set pieces.
